content.full_text:
June 21, 1976

Mr. Thomas H. Gordinier
Deputy County Counsel
County of Solano
County Court House
Fairfield, California 94533

Dear Mr. Gordinier:

This letter is in response to your letter of May 18,
and our telephone conversation of some days thereafter.

District attorneys are required to file Statements of
Economic Interests pursuant to Government Code §87200 with
the Fair Political Practices Commission. A district attorney is not covered by any Conflict of Interest Code for
which the Fair Political Practices Commission is a code
reviewing body. However, a district attorney is covered
in a Conflict of Interest Code for the office of the
district attorney; this Conflict of Interest Code is
reviewed by the county board of supervisors. However,
the district attorney is not required to disclose pursuant
to that Code, since all §7200 filers are exempt by Government Code §87302(b) from the requirement that they disclose
pursuant to a Conflict of Interest Code. In other words,
the only portion of a Conflict of Interest Code which
governs the actions of a district attorney are those
portions requiring disqualification under certain circumstances.

You also inquire whether members of boards of supervisors who serve as officers of local government agencies
with jurisdiction in more than one county would have to
file different Statements of Economic Interests with the
multi-county agency upon which they serve. The answer is
yes. Pursuant to Government Code §87200, a member of a
board of supervisors files a Statement of Economic Interests
disclosing investments in business entities and interest in
real property within the jurisdiction. As a member of the


Mr. Thomas H. Gardinier
June 21, 1976 - Page Two


board of supervisors, the "jurisdiction" is the county.
However, where the member of the board of supervisors
sits as a member of a multi-county agency, the juris-
diction for disclosure of investments in business
entities and interest in real property under a Conflict
of Interest Code would coincide with the jurisdiction
of the multi-county agency and require a filing differ-
ent than that required for the filing pursuant to §87200.

As I mentioned in our conversation, one way to
alleviate the problem of multiple disclosure statements
would be to inform the members of the board of super-
visors that they may, in effect, over-disclose with
respect to their original filing as members of the
board of supervisors and disclose all investments in
business entities and interest in real property in the
State of California. Thus, they could satisfy the
disclosure requirements of a Conflict of Interest
Code for a multi-county agency merely by filing a
copy of the Statement of Economic Interests submitted
to the Fair Political Practices Commission under
Government Code §87200 et seq. Naturally, members
of the board of supervisors could not be compelled to
engage in such over-disclosure, but I feel that they
may engage in such over-disclosure; and, by mixing
that they have done so on their original filing,
alleviate any misunderstandings on the part of other
agency officials or interested persons who may be
reviewing their Statements of Economic Interests.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr.
                                        Chief, Conflicts of Interest
                                        Division


DLS/gb


OFFICE OF
COUNTY COUNSEL
County Court House
FAIRFIELD, CALIFORNIA 94533
(707) 429-6491

MILTON GOLDINGER
COUNTY COUNSEL

BILLY P. BURTON
ASSISTANT COUNTY COUNSEL
HARRY B. WYETH
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL
J. PAUL COAN
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL
THOMAS H. GORDINIER
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL
PAUL J. FOLEY, JR.
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL

May 18, 1976

Ms. Dawn Wiser
Enforcement/Conflict of
Interest Section
State Fair Political
Practices Commission
1100 K Street Building
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Wiser:

Re: Political Reform Act of 1974

This is to confirm our telephone conversation of May 17, 1976
wherein the undersigned discussed with you and an attorney on
your staff the reporting requirements for a District Attorney.

It is our understanding that a District Attorney is required
to file a Statement of Economic Interest, as required by
Government Code §87202, which must be filed within thirty (30)
days after the anniversary of his taking his oath of office.
The District Attorney does not have to provide the FPPC with a
disqualification code or declaration.

The District Attorney is required to prepare and adopt a con-
flict of interest code, as required under Government Code §87300,
for approval by the County Board of Supervisors. This Code may
be brief, in that he is only responsible to report matters not
covered in the Statement of Economic Interest, and not required to
meet the full disclosure requirements of §87300, et seq. He
does not have to file a financial disclosure statement with the
County Board of Supervisors as provided for in §87300.

Although the following was not discussed in the above-referenced
telephone conversation, it is also our understanding that members
of the County Board of Supervisors, as specified under §87200, must
file a statement of economic interest on the anniversary of their
having taken their oath of office. The Board of Supervisors is also


MS.: Dawn Wiser
State FPPC
Re: Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
May 18, 1976 - Page 2 (THG:gg)

--------------------------------------

required to submit a Conflict of Interest Code to the FPPC for
approval, per §87300. Where a member of the Board of Supervisors
also serves as a director of another local agency, he would be
required to file a financial disclosure statement under §87300,
with either the FPPC or the County Clerk, depending on the scope
of the local agency. The initial financial disclosure statement
required would be due within thirty (30) days after the conflict of interest code was adopted by the code-reviewing body.
However, subsequent to filing the initial statement, copies of an
annual disclosure statement filed with one agency, may be filed
with all other governmental agencies instead of making original
declarations for each agency.

It is requested that after your review of this letter, you provide written concurrence or dissent as to the interpretations indicated by this office.

Thank you for your kind considerations and patience in this matter.

Very truly yours,

MILTON GOLDINGER
County Counsel

[signature]
THOMAS H. GORDINER
Deputy County Counsel

MG:THG:gg
